preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatalized labial segments) into the voiced labiodental fricative [v] is a form of labialization and consonantalization, notably documented in historical sound changes where rounded vowels harden into fricatives.

examples.note: Shift where historical labialized vowels or glides fortified into the voiced labiodental fricative in the development of various daughter languages.
Documentation of rounded vowels shifting toward labial approximants or fricatives before potentially hardening into stops.
